title: Compare decimals (tenths and hundredths)
description: Practice comparing decimals. Decimals in these problems are limited to tenths and hundredths.
topic_path: Get ready for decimal place value

hints:
$<$ means "is less than."

$>$ means "is greater than."

$=$ means "is the same as."

First, let's use a place value table to turn $\blueD{25 \text{ hundredths}}$ into standard form.

The last place value in the number is *hundredths*, so the number ends in the *hundredths* place.

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$\blueD0$ |$.$  | $\blueD2$ | $\blueD5$

Now let's use the place value table to compare our two numbers.

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$\blueD0$ |$.$  | $\blueD2$ | $\blueD5$ 
$\greenD0$| $.$  | $\greenD3$ | $\greenD8$

The greatest place value with different digits is the tenths.  

$\blueD{0.25}$ has $\blueD2$ tenths.  $\greenD{0.38}$ has $\greenD3$ tenths.

Since $\blueD{0.25}$ has less tenths, it is less than $\greenD{0.38}$.
$\blueD{25\text{ hundredths}} < \greenD{0.38}$

$<$ means "is less than."

$>$ means "is greater than."

$=$ means "is the same as."

First, let's use a place value table to turn $\greenD{5 \text{ tenths and }4\text{ hundredths}}$ into standard form.

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$\greenD0$ |$.$  | $\greenD5$ | $\greenD4$

Now let's use the place value table to compare our two numbers.

Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: 
$\blueD0$ |$.$  | $\blueD8$ | $\blueD2$ 
$\greenD0$| $.$  | $\greenD5$ | $\greenD4$

The greatest place value with different digits is the tenths.  

$\blueD{0.82}$ has $\blueD8$ tenths.  $\greenD{0.54}$ has $\greenD5$ tenths.

Since $\blueD{0.82}$ has more tenths, it is greater than $\greenD{0.54}$.
$\blueD{0.82} > \greenD{5\text{ tenths and }4\text{ hundredths}}$

$<$ means "is less than."

$>$ means "is greater than."

$=$ means "is the same as."

$\greenD0$| $.$  | $\greenD8$ | $\greenD8$
All the digits in $\blueD{88\text{ hundredths}}$ and $\greenD{0.88}$ are the same so they are equal.
$\blueD{88\text{ hundredths}} = \greenD{0.88}$

The greatest place value with different digits is the ones.  

$\blueD{0.73}$ has $\blueD0$ ones.  $\greenD{1.71}$ has $\greenD1$ one.

Since $\blueD{0.73}$ has less ones, it is less than $\greenD{1.71}$.
